sections.paragraph:
- Original: σκήνωμα

- Transliteration: Skenoma

- Phonetic: skay'-no-mah

- Definition:

1. a tent, a tabernacle

a. of the temple as God's habitation

b. of the tabernacle of the covenant

c. metaph. of the human body as the dwelling of the soul

- Origin: from G4637

- TDNT entry: 13:23,1

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Neuter

- Strong's: From G4637; an encampment that is (figuratively) the temple (as God´ s residence) the body (as a tenement for the soul): - tabernacle.
